pi_items.source: Task description specifies saving annotated recording as 'case_annotated.vital' in Documents folder. This is the expected output filename.
Task description explicitly requires adding event markers labeled 'Max HR' and 'Min MAP'. Verifier checks for these exact event names in the .vital file.

Verifier code (line 117) filters MAP data with: valid_indices = np.where(map_data > 30)[0]. Analysis of 0001.vital shows data values range from 53.0-346.0 mmHg when filtered at >30 threshold, confirming this is the correct artifact filtering threshold for physiological MAP measurements.

Verifier code (lines 92, 122) uses this threshold: if diff <= 30.0 for full score, elif diff <= 60.0 for partial score. This is the tolerance window for comparing agent-placed event times against ground truth values.
Direct analysis of 0001.vital: Solar8000/HR track contains 5455 valid values (>0). Maximum value is 139.0 bpm occurring at 10721.0 seconds (2h 58m 41s into the recording).

Direct analysis of 0001.vital using vitaldb library. Computed using: valid_indices = np.where(hr_data > 0)[0]; true_max_idx = valid_indices[np.argmax(hr_data[valid_indices])]; true_max_time = true_max_idx * 1.0 (1 second sampling interval).
Direct analysis of 0001.vital: Solar8000/ART_MBP track contains 5063 valid values (>30). Minimum physiological value is 53.0 mmHg after filtering artifacts.

Direct analysis of 0001.vital using vitaldb library. Computed using: valid_indices = np.where(map_data > 30)[0]; true_min_idx = valid_indices[np.argmin(map_data[valid_indices])]; true_min_time = true_min_idx * 1.0 (1 second sampling interval).

pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB - real surgical cases from Seoul National University Hospital (6,388 cases recorded Aug 2016 - Jun 2017)
pi_items.source: Environment description mentions 'VitalDB open dataset'. Verified through PMC/NIH publication (PMCID: PMC9178032): Data consists of 'automatically recorded by the Vital Recorder program during daily surgery and anesthesia' at Seoul National University Hospital. All cases were manually verified by four anesthesiologists.

PhysioNet VitalDB documentation: 'random surgery case identifiers (caseid)' were assigned to cases numbered 1–6,388. File 0001.vital corresponds to caseid=1, referred to as Case #1 in task description.

privileged_info_summary: The task annotate_hemodynamic_extremes operates on Case #1 (0001.vital) from the VitalDB open dataset, containing real surgical vital signs data from Seoul National University Hospital (2016-2017). Ground truth measurements: Maximum Heart Rate is 139.0 bpm at 10721.0 seconds; Minimum Mean Arterial Pressure (>30 mmHg threshold) is 53.0 mmHg at 4615.0 seconds. Metadata values (expected_filename, expected_events, min_map_threshold: 30, tolerance_seconds: 30) are correctly specified for verifying agent task completion.
